guidelines.usage: Use as the default surface behind all content. Light: white (#FFFFFF). Dark: near-black (#202020).

guidelines.description: Primary text color on the background
guidelines.usage: Use for body text, headings, and icon fills rendered directly on the background surface.
Use as the background for Card, Dialog, Sheet, and similar elevated surfaces.
Use for all text and icon content rendered inside Card components.

guidelines.description: Background for dropdown menus and popovers
guidelines.usage: Use as the background for Popover, DropdownMenu, Tooltip, and Select content surfaces.
Use for all text and icon content inside Popover and DropdownMenu components.

guidelines.description: Brand color for primary actions (buttons, highlights)
guidelines.usage: Use for primary Button backgrounds, active tab indicators, and key interactive highlights. This is the main brand action color.

guidelines.description: Text and icons on primary-colored elements
guidelines.usage: Use for text and icon content rendered on top of primary-colored surfaces (e.g., label inside a primary button).
Use for secondary Button backgrounds and less-prominent interactive surfaces.

guidelines.description: Text and icons on secondary-colored elements
guidelines.usage: Use for text and icons rendered on top of secondary surfaces.

guidelines.description: Subdued background for less important elements
guidelines.usage: Use for muted badges, skeleton loaders, disabled input backgrounds, and code block surfaces.
Use for placeholder text, captions, helper text, and secondary labels that should recede visually.

guidelines.description: Highlight color for active or focused elements
guidelines.usage: Use for hovered or active row/item backgrounds in lists, menus, and navigation.

guidelines.description: Text and icons on accent-colored elements
guidelines.usage: Use for text and icons rendered on accent-background surfaces.
Use for error states, delete confirmation dialogs, and destructive action buttons.

guidelines.description: Text and icons on destructive-colored elements
guidelines.usage: Use for text and icons rendered on destructive backgrounds.
Use for dividers, card outlines, table row separators, and general-purpose borders.
Use for the border of text inputs, select fields, textareas, and icon-button outlines in their default (unfocused) state.
Use as the outline or box-shadow ring rendered around focused interactive elements for accessibility.

guidelines.description: The color of the space between a focused element and its ring
guidelines.usage: Use as the background-color beneath the focus ring offset, so the ring appears visually separated from the focused element.
Use as the background of the application sidebar panel.
Use for all text and icon content inside the sidebar.
Use for active navigation items and primary interactive elements within the sidebar.
Use for text and icons on top of sidebar-primary-colored backgrounds.
Use for hovered or focused navigation item backgrounds within the sidebar.
Use for text and icons on top of sidebar-accent-colored backgrounds.
Use for horizontal or vertical separators within the sidebar.
Use for focus rings on interactive elements inside the sidebar.
Use for the first data series in charts, graphs, and data visualizations.
Use for the second data series in charts and graphs.
Use for the third data series in charts and graphs.
Use for the fourth data series in charts and graphs.
Use for the fifth data series in charts and graphs.
Use success-background, success-border, success-icon, success-primary-text, and success-secondary-text together in alert, toast, and banner components for positive confirmations.
Use error-background, error-border, error-icon, and error-*-text together in alert, form validation, and toast components for failure states.
Use warning-background, warning-border, warning-icon, and warning-*-text together for cautionary alerts and non-blocking issues.
Use caution-* tokens for lower-severity warnings such as expiring items or soft deadlines.
Use info-* tokens for neutral informational messages, help text callouts, and instructional banners.
Use passive-* tokens for disabled or inactive status indicators that carry no urgency.

Use alpha/* tokens as scrim overlays on modal backdrops, skeleton loaders, or layered surfaces. Light mode values are white-based; dark mode values are black-based. Pick the opacity level by number (5 = most transparent, 90 = most opaque).

guidelines.description: Spatial scale based on a 4px base unit (Tailwind-compatible)
guidelines.usage: Use spacing tokens for padding, margin, gap, and inset values. Each step multiplies the base: spacing/1 = 4px, spacing/2 = 8px, spacing/4 = 16px, etc. Use spacing/px for 1px hairline offsets.

Match the component size to its rounding:
• rounded-none (0px) — sharp corners, data tables.
• rounded-sm (2px) — badges, tags.
• rounded-md (6px) — inputs, buttons.
• rounded-lg (10px) — cards, modals.
• rounded-xl / 2xl — elevated panels.
• rounded-3xl / 4xl — pill-shaped containers.
• rounded-full (9999px) — circular avatars, pill buttons.

Mariner is the core brand color. Use mariner/600 (#0069B4) for primary interactive elements in light mode. Use mariner/400 (#5EACE0) in dark mode. Lighter shades (50–300) work as backgrounds and tints; darker shades (700–950) for pressed states or high-contrast contexts.

guidelines.description: Wagyu — the brand red / destructive accent
guidelines.usage: Wagyu is the secondary brand color used for warnings, error highlights, and brand accents. Use wagyu/600 (#DB3327) for destructive actions. Lighter shades for backgrounds and tints.

Angus provides the grey ramp for text, borders, backgrounds, and muted surfaces. Use angus/950 (#1F1F21) for dark-mode backgrounds and primary text in light mode. Use angus/100 (#EEEEF0) for light-mode muted surfaces.

guidelines.description: Top navigation bar surface and foreground
guidelines.usage: Use nav-top-background as the AppBar/topbar fill and nav-top-foreground for text and icon content within it.

guidelines.description: Bottom navigation bar surface and foreground
guidelines.usage: Use for mobile bottom-tab navigation bars.
Use as the semi-transparent overlay behind modal dialogs and drawers.
Apply as the ring color on focusable elements that use a custom focus style rather than the default base/ring token.
